title: Providence Isn’t an Official World Cup Host but It Sure Is Acting Like One
description: A stadium outside Boston that will host seven World Cup games is actually closer to Providence, R.I., prompting officials there to capitalize on that proximity to lure fans.
author: Adeel Hassan

title: Trans Athlete Could Repeat as State Champion at California Meet
description: Special rules will again be in place. Podium spots might be shared.
author: Juliet Macur

title: California House Primary in Sacramento Displays Democrats’ Fierce Generational Battle
description: Representative Doris Matsui has been re-elected 10 times with ease. But an upstart Democrat is threatening to end her run in Congress.
author: Laurel Rosenhall

title: Why Hasn’t California Elected a Woman Governor?
description: Most states have had a woman serve as governor, but California is in the minority despite its progressive-minded electorate. Women who have reached the top elsewhere saw familiar roadblocks.
author: Heather Knight, Soumya Karlamangla and Jill Cowan

title: Scripps Spelling Bee 2026 Takeaways: Shrey Parikh Wins in Spell-Off
description: The competition came down to a spell-off. Contestants were eliminated early, and a sixth grader became a crowd favorite.
author: Maria Cramer

title: 8 Are Rescued From Texas Roller Coaster Stuck Almost 100 Feet Off the Ground
description: The riders waited for about four hours, facing up, on the roller coaster at the Pleasure Pier park in Galveston. There were no injuries, the authorities said.
author: Francesca Regalado

title: Here’s the latest.
author: Max Bearak

title: In Carroll Lawsuits Inquiry, Scrutiny Turns Toward Private Citizens Who Antagonized Trump
description: The Justice Department is said to be examining the funding of lawsuits brought by E. Jean Carroll, an author who has never sought a public role, political power or governmental authority.
author: Glenn Thrush and Benjamin Weiser

title: Where Do E. Jean Carroll’s Lawsuits Against President Trump Stand?
description: Two monetary judgments against Mr. Trump are winding their way through the legal system, with the Supreme Court likely to be the final word on both.
author: Abbie VanSickle

title: Families Settle Suit Over Deaths in Baltimore Bridge Collapse
description: The owner and the operator of the cargo ship that crashed into the Key Bridge settled cases brought by several victims’ families, but other claims are headed to trial.
author: Campbell Robertson

title: 3 Dead After Large Fire at Dallas Apartment Building
description: A damaged gas line led to a five-alarm fire in the Oak Cliff neighborhood. At least four were injured, and firefighters believe they may find more victims.
author: Jesus Jiménez

title: Alaska Authorities Race to Rescue 4 Climbers Who Fell on Mount McKinley
description: The condition of the climbers was not immediately known.
author: Alexandra E. Petri

title: Michigan Governor Gretchen Whitmer Will Not Run for President in 2028
description: The Michigan governor made the remarks to a reporter on Thursday morning. Later, she sounded less definitive. Ms. Whitmer has been viewed as part of a field of possible Democratic candidates.
author: Mitch Smith

title: Judge Declines, for Now, to Block Mail-In Voting Changes Ordered by Trump
description: The ruling allowed the Trump administration to continue to pursue changes that include enlisting the Postal Service in checking voters against a national database.
author: Zach Montague and Adam Sella

title: J.F.K. Airport to Begin Screening Some Passengers for Ebola
description: The U.S. has begun rerouting travelers from the Democratic Republic of Congo, South Sudan and Uganda to select airports for enhanced health screenings, to identify those who may have been exposed to the Ebola virus.
author: Karoun Demirjian

title: After Traffic Stop for Phone in Right Hand, She Called Out the Problem
description: The deputy insisted he saw a driver with a phone in her right hand. That turned out to be impossible, and the case was dropped.
author: Mark Walker

title: Suit Says Black Infants Were Subjected to Experimental Vaccine Without Consent
description: The babies were part of a vaccine trial for a respiratory virus in the 1960s and died shortly after. Their families said they had been unaware of the trial until recently.
author: Remy Tumin

title: Death Toll Rises in Washington Paper Mill Explosion
description: At least eight are now confirmed dead, and firefighters believe there are three more bodies they have yet to reach, making the crisis the worst industrial disaster in the state’s modern history.
author: Anna Griffin and Aaron West

title: Trump’s Iran Approach Sows Confusion
description: President Trump’s pendulum swings on Iran have often seemed driven by mood and moment rather than any discernible strategy.
author: Michael Crowley and Eric Schmitt

title: Who Are the 9 Scripps Spelling Bee Finalists?
description: The remaining spellers are 12 to 14, and represent five states as well as the District of Columbia.
author: Ashley Ahn
